name: Irving Mills
description: American music publisher, singer, lyricist, and jazz artist promoter (1894-1985)
abstract: Irving Harold Mills (born Isadore Minsky; January 18, 1894 Odessa, Ukraine – April 21, 1985) was a Ukrainian-American music publisher, musician, lyricist, and jazz promoter. He often used the pseudonyms Goody Goodwin and Joe Primrose.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. Well look at Irving Mills and Duke Ellington. ... [INTERVIEWEE] At least there the personalities of the historical figures are well enough known that you can pretty well guess as to what’s going on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: He [my uncle Sid Phillips] had gone to New York for Irving Mills... Irving Mills, who had his publishing, invited him to New York
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: I mean he didn’t have an Irving Mills like Ellington had or Cab Calloway, somebody like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Obviously [Duke] Ellington felt the same way – Irving Mills. They could get things done at a time when blacks were not taken very seriously.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: He traveled with us. Everything first class... he said 'I better get you guys another set of uniforms'.

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Oakley Dance
about_quotes.evidence: I was brought into Mills’ office and that was the other half of my career... I was the producer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Helen Oakley Dance interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998
